items.title: Aboriginal leaders call for calm in Alice as police warn against 'vigilante justice'

children.content: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

articles.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested

children.content: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A person picking up a smoke canister on the street at night with crowds in the background.

articles.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Jefferson Lewis has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering the young girl in Alice Springs.

image.altText: Flowers on a fence

gallery.altText: A man stands inside a house.
Riot police outside the Alice Springs Hospital
A woman gestures towards the hospital after appearing to start a fire under a police car

Riot police outside the Alice Springs Hospital
A woman gestures towards the hospital after appearing to start a fire under a police car

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to be taken from her bed in the night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be and what it is like to live in one.

image.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

items.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for man who killed her

children.content: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

image.altText: A family stands in front of the NT Supreme Court looking sad and wearing Justice for Lena shirts.

items.title: Chinese company launches legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin

children.content: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.

gallery.altText: A large piece of equipment on the port.

thumbnail.altText: A large piece of equipment on the port.

items.title: Tough-on-crime NT government snubs youth incarceration Senate inquiry

children.content: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: A group of young Indigenous children are playing basketball inside the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre.

items.title: NT government accused of hiding long-term watch house detentions

children.content: Just weeks after the CLP government insisted the Northern Territory's watch house crisis was over, the NT's Aboriginal justice agency has accused it of renaming parts of a Darwin watch house to conceal that people are still being held there long term.

image.altText: Low-angle shot of Darwin city watch house building, with blue and white checkered tiles on front of building in view.

items.title: Darwin lord mayor defends proposed 5.5 per cent rates rise

children.content: In his first council budget, Darwin lord mayor Peter Styles has proposed a rates hike which he says he believes people will agree with despite no new major projects being on the cards.

image.altText: Lord Mayor Peter Styles wears a suit and looks sternly past the camera.

items.title: NT paramedics 'beaten' to the point of 'semi-consciousness'

children.content: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

image.altText: An ambulance pulled side of road at dusk in city

items.title: Rare quadruplet birth stuns mum, 45, and doctors

children.content: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

gallery.altText: A man and woman cradling two newborns, with another two in cribs behind them
A man and woman cradling two newborns

items.title: Cases of potentially deadly disease triple in the NT

children.content: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

image.altText: A needle injecting a vaccine into a patient's arm.

items.title: Mother haunted by letters after two-year-old son killed in murder-suicide

children.content: A coronial inquest hears letters that arrived three days after an apparent murder-suicide in Lismore outlined a father's deadly plans.

image.altText: A smiling, blonde little boy sitting on a chair holding a cup.

items.title: Beaches evacuated as sharks swarm towards 25-tonne whale carcass

children.content: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

image.altText: image of boat ramp with boat to left, dead whale in centre and a digger hoisting it up.

items.title: Australian 'ISIS brides' in limbo after reportedly being blocked from reaching Syrian airport

children.content: A week since a group of 13 Australian women and children with ties to former Islamic State fighters left a Syrian refugee camp for Australia, they remain in the capital Damascus in a state of limbo.

image.altText: A young girl and three women walking towards a mini bus.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

gallery.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

thumbnail.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

items.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute as Freo downs Dogs

children.content: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

image.altText: A group of AFL players celebrate a victory in front of crowd

items.title: Melbourne Storm lose seven in a row for the first time in club history

children.content: The Melbourne Storm have sunk to a historic low, losing seven consecutive NRL matches for the first time as the Dolphins powered to victory at a wet Lang Park in Brisbane.

image.altText: NRL players catch their breath behind the goal-line after the opposition score

items.title: Actor on Rebel Wilson film accused of breaching NDA, court hears

children.content: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

image.altText: Charlotte MacInnes arrives at Federal Court of Australia

items.title: Lessons should have been learnt prior to NDIS rollout, integrity boss says

children.content: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

image.altText: A close up of an NDIS sign next to a building

items.title: Is the new Foo Fighters the best album of April?

children.content: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a burger blue and a rescued roo

children.content: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

image.altText: Secret service agents respond during the incident at the White House correspondents' dinner.

items.title: Australian screen industry as 'intergenerational' as housing

children.content: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

image.altText: A man stands behind a podium and in front of a projector

items.title: The battery rebate became a victim of its success. Here's what is changing

children.content: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

gallery.altText: Leanne Phillips stands next to a work truck in a driveway in Toowoomba, April 2026.

thumbnail.altText: Leanne Phillips stands next to a work truck in a driveway in Toowoomba, April 2026.

items.title: Bondi royal commission interim report careful but ultimately damning

children.content: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

image.altText: Police officers at Bondi Beach seven days after terrorist attack

items.title: Melbourne teacher accused of installing bathroom camera denied bail

children.content: Parents of children attending Kingswood Primary School filled the Moorabbin Magistrates Court on Friday, intending to see accused teacher Timothy Blamires.

image.altText: Timothy Blamires

items.title: Banksy behind overnight reveal of new statue in central London

children.content: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

gallery.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.

thumbnail.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.
